title: Your first tournament: a survival guide
microFact: Target a Stableford event via 4moles or a corporate day. Your only goals: post a score, make three friends, get invited back.
lesson:
What actually happens at an amateur event in India:

BEFORE: entry via the organizer (4moles.com lists Bangalore events monthly; your credit-card golf program runs them too). You'll give your IGU Handicap Index. Arrive 1 hour early: registration desk, goodie bag, breakfast, range balls.

FORMAT: almost always individual Stableford (byte 30) with a shotgun start — all groups tee off simultaneously on different holes ("you're starting on 14").

DURING: you're paired with strangers — that's the point. Introduce yourself, announce your ball, keep your marker's card diligently, play ready golf, pick up when you can't score points. Nobody expects a 28-handicap to play well; they expect exactly the etiquette you've been learning for six weeks.

AFTER: return your card SIGNED, then stay for lunch and prizes. Leaving straight after the round is the rookie exit — the entire networking value of the day is at the table.

Realistic goals, in order: finish with a legal card, keep pace, learn three names and follow up, enjoy it. Points are a bonus. Invitations compound.

quiz.question: What is a 'shotgun start'?
options: All groups tee off at the same time on different holes
quiz.explanation: Every group starts simultaneously around the course, so everyone finishes together — standard for tournaments with meals after.
